name: Canton Chicken & Snow Peas
recipeIngredient: 1.5 pounds Chicken breasts, skinned, boned, and cut into bite-sized pieces
¼ pound Snow peas, ends and strings removed
recipeInstructions:
1. Cover mushrooms with warm water, let stand for 30 minutes, then drain. Cut off and discard stems; squeeze mushrooms dry, thinly slice and set aside.
2. In a bowl, combine soy sauce, cornstarch, sherry, water, and pepper. Add chicken and toss to coat. Stir in 1.5 tablespoons of the oil and let stand for fifteen minutes to marinate.
3. Prepare cooking sauce by mixing together water, sherry, oyster sauce, sugar, sesame oil, and cornstarch. Set aside.
4. Place a wok or wide frying pan over high heat. When pan is hot, add 3.5 tablespoons of the oil. When oil begins to heat, add garlic and stir once.
5. Add chicken and stir-fry until chicken is opaque (about 3 minutes). Remove chicken from pan.
6. Add remaining 3.5 tablespoons of oil to pan. When oil is hot, add mushrooms and bamboo shoots. Stir-fry for one minute, adding a few drops water if the pan appears dry.
7. Add snow peas and stir-fry for 1.5 minutes, adding a few drops more water if the pan appears dry.
8. Return chicken to pan. Stir in cooking sauce, add to pan, and cook, stirring until sauce bubbles and thickens.
Variations:
- Cashew or Almond Chicken: First toast ½ cup cashews or blanched almonds in 1 tablespoon salad oil over medium-low heat until golden; remove from pan and set aside. Stir in nuts just before serving.
